task: Quick cleanup: find and fix any obvious issues in files changed in the last 3 git commits: typos in user-facing text, missing html.escape calls, hardcoded colors that should use CSS variables.

assignments.frontend: Review files changed in the last 3 git commits for hardcoded hex colors (e.g. #fff, #1a1a2e) that should use CSS variables from components.py :root block. Fix any found. Run: git diff HEAD~3 --name-only to get the file list.
assignments.backend: Review files changed in the last 3 git commits for missing html.escape() calls on user-supplied data injected into f-string HTML templates (tool names, descriptions, usernames, etc). Fix any found. Run: git diff HEAD~3 --name-only to get the file list.
assignments.content: Review files changed in the last 3 git commits for typos in user-facing text — button labels, headings, paragraphs, meta descriptions, error messages. Fix any found. Run: git diff HEAD~3 --name-only to get the file list.

decomposition.reasoning: Three independent concern areas (CSS variables, XSS safety, copy quality) that can all scan the same set of recently-changed files in parallel. Each department focuses on their domain expertise. No DevOps or MCP work needed — this is purely a code quality sweep.

strategy_review.reasoning: Well-scoped maintenance task with a tight blast radius (3 commits). The html.escape audit is genuinely important — missing escapes are XSS vectors on a public site. Hardcoded colors and typos are low-risk but cheap to fix while we're already looking. Three departments is slightly heavy for 'quick cleanup' but each concern requires different expertise (security awareness vs design system knowledge vs copy-editing), so the split is defensible. Past runs show single-department tasks cost $0.05-$0.19, so three in parallel should land around $0.50-$0.80 — acceptable for a security+hygiene pass.

approved_tasks.frontend: Review files changed in the last 3 git commits for hardcoded hex colors (e.g. #fff, #1a1a2e) that should use CSS variables from components.py :root block. Fix any found. Run: git diff HEAD~3 --name-only to get the file list.
approved_tasks.backend: Review files changed in the last 3 git commits for missing html.escape() calls on user-supplied data injected into f-string HTML templates (tool names, descriptions, usernames, etc). Fix any found. Run: git diff HEAD~3 --name-only to get the file list.
approved_tasks.content: Review files changed in the last 3 git commits for typos in user-facing text — button labels, headings, paragraphs, meta descriptions, error messages. Fix any found. Run: git diff HEAD~3 --name-only to get the file list.

conditions: Do NOT commit changes — leave them staged or unstaged so Patrick can review the full diff before committing. Three departments editing overlapping files risks conflicts if they commit independently.
Backend department: only flag html.escape issues on genuinely user-supplied data (tool names from DB, search queries, usernames). Don't wrap static strings or internal constants — that's noise.
risk_flags: All three departments will touch the same file list. If two departments edit the same line differently, the second edit will fail. Frontend should run first (colors are least likely to conflict with content/backend changes).
Be careful not to over-escape — double-escaping already-escaped content is a common mistake that produces visible & artifacts on the page.
